step_by_step_thinking:
Given that the patient went into shock immediately following a normal delivery, we should focus on causes that could lead to rapid onset hypotension and cardiovascular collapse. Amniotic Fluid Embolism (AFE) stands out because it involves sudden pulmonary vasodilation leading to acute right heart dysfunction followed by left ventricular failure due to systemic hypoperfusion, often resulting in cardiac arrest if untreated promptly. Postpartum Hemorrhage (PPH) typically presents later but can occur acutely depending on severity. Uterine inversion would also present late unless there was trauma during delivery. Eclampsia usually occurs intra-partum or shortly thereafter rather than immediately post-delivery.
\boxed{A} Based on the symptoms described and considering the rapid onset of shock, Amniotic Fluid Embolism (AFE) appears to be the most plausible explanation given its characteristic features of sudden hemodynamic instability and respiratory compromise.
